name: Arcutis Biotherapeutics, Inc.
business: We are a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing treatments for dermatological diseases with high unmet medical needs. Our current portfolio is comprised of highly differentiated topical and systemic treatments with significant potential to treat immune-mediated dermatological diseases and conditions. Our lead product, ZORYVE cream 0.3%, is approved for once-daily topical treatment of mild, moderate, and severe plaque psoriasis with no limitations on location or duration of use, and we have additional approved products for seborrheic dermatitis and atopic dermatitis.

exposures.sentence: We are a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing treatments for dermatological diseases with high unmet medical needs.

catalysts.event: PDUFA target action date for ZORYVE cream 0.3% in plaque psoriasis down to age 2
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, our supplemental New Drug Application (sNDA) was accepted for filing by the FDA to potentially expand the indication of ZORYVE cream 0.3% for the treatment of plaque psoriasis in children down to the age of 2, with a Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) target action date set for June 29, 2026.

catalysts.event: Planned sNDA submission for ZORYVE cream 0.05% for atopic dermatitis in infants down to 3 months
catalysts.sentence: We intend to submit an sNDA to the FDA in the second quarter of 2026 based on the results of this trial to potentially expand the indication for ZORYVE cream 0.05% for the treatment of infants with atopic dermatitis down to the age of 3 months.

catalysts.event: Anticipated commencement of Phase 1 study for ARQ-234
catalysts.sentence: We submitted an Investigational New Drug application (IND) to the FDA in July 2025, and anticipate commencing a Phase 1 study of ARQ-234 in the first quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Termination of promotion agreement with Kowa Pharmaceuticals America
catalysts.sentence: Effective January 23, 2026, we mutually agreed to terminate the promotion agreement.

catalysts.event: Health Canada acceptance of SNDS for ZORYVE cream 0.3% down to age 2
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, Health Canada accepted our Supplement to a New Drug Submission (SNDS) for ZORYVE cream 0.3% for individuals down to 2 years old.

catalysts.event: Positive topline data for INTEGUMENT-INFANT Phase 2 study
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we announced positive topline data for INTEGUMENT-INFANT, a Phase 2 study to evaluate the safety and efficacy of investigational ZORYVE cream 0.05% in infants as young as 3 months to less than 2 years with atopic dermatitis.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 17 million actively prescription-treated patients in the United States for plaque psoriasis, seborrheic dermatitis, and atopic dermatitis.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on market research and our internal estimates, we estimate there is an overall actively prescription-treated patient market of approximately 17.0 million patients in the United States that are treated with topical therapies for plaque psoriasis, seborrheic dermatitis, and atopic dermatitis.

tamClaims.claim: Psoriasis affects about 9 million people in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Psoriasis is an immune disease that occurs in about 3% of the U.S. population, representing approximately 9 million patients.

tamClaims.claim: Atopic dermatitis affects approximately 26 million people in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Atopic dermatitis is the most common type of eczema, affecting approximately 26 million people in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Seborrheic dermatitis affects more than 10 million people in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Seborrheic dermatitis is a common skin disease that is estimated to occur in more than 10 million people in the United States.
